summary: Comprehensive testing of Gender/Rainbow visibility features. All 15 backend tests passed. Frontend UI verified for OnboardingGender page, ProfileTab Gender & Identity section, and Discovery/WhosHere PersonCard indicators.

tests: check_visibility_match() function correctly implements bidirectional gender matching
Non-rainbow users only see non-rainbow users
Rainbow users can see both but only appear to other rainbow users
Visibility filter applied in /discovery/not-here, /discovery/here, and /venues/{id}/people endpoints
Invalid show_as values are rejected with 400 error
Changing show_as resets seeking and intent fields
seeking values are normalized to lowercase
rainbow toggle can be set to true
rainbow toggle can be set to false

ui_elements: Title: 'How would you like to appear?'
I'm looking to meet: Men/Women multi-select buttons (data-testid='seeking-male-btn', 'seeking-female-btn')
Rainbow toggle with correct label '🌈 Rainbow / Rainbow-friendly'
Rainbow description: 'I'm LGBTQ+ and/or open to seeing LGBTQ+ people.'
Gender change warning toast appears when show_as is changed

implementation: Gender indicator (M in blue / F in pink) at bottom-left of photo
Rainbow arc icon at bottom-right of photo (gradient background)
Implemented in both Discovery.js and WhosHere.js PersonCard components

critical_code_review_comments: check_visibility_match() function in dependencies.py (line 761-813) correctly implements bidirectional visibility rules
WhoIsHereUser model includes show_as and rainbow fields
Profile update in auth.py validates show_as and resets seeking/intent on change
OnboardingGender.js correctly saves show_as to backend via /auth/profile
ProfileTab.js Gender & Identity section positioned before Connection Preferences
PersonCard in Discovery.js and WhosHere.js includes gender and rainbow indicators with correct styling
context_for_next_testing_agent: Gender/Rainbow visibility features fully implemented and tested. Backend check_visibility_match() function applies bidirectional gender matching and rainbow boundary rules. Frontend has OnboardingGender page for new users, ProfileTab Gender & Identity section with show_as/seeking/rainbow controls, and PersonCard indicators in Discovery and WhosHere pages. All data-testid attributes are in place for automated testing.
